title: Structure of the NSDAP
description: The NSDAP was organised as a highly centralised party with the Führer at its apex. The party structure paralleled and ultimately superseded the German state apparatus.

hierarchy.description: Adolf Hitler. Held absolute authority over the party. After 1934, combined party leadership with the offices of Chancellor and President.
The central party apparatus in Munich. Consisted of Reichsleiters responsible for specific functions: propaganda (Goebbels), finance (Schwarz), party organisation (Ley), racial policy (Rosenberg).

Germany was divided into 43 Gaue (districts). Each Gauleiter was a regional party boss with considerable local authority, often serving simultaneously as Reichsstatthalter (Reich Governor) of their territory.
Each Gau was divided into Kreise (counties). Kreisleiter were responsible for party organisation and implementing directives at county level.
The base level of the party organisation, covering villages and urban neighbourhoods. Responsible for surveillance, welfare distribution, and enforcing conformity.

affiliated_organisations.name: Sturmabteilung (SA)
affiliated_organisations.role: Original paramilitary wing, 'Brownshirts'. Subordinated after Night of the Long Knives (1934).

affiliated_organisations.name: Schutzstaffel (SS)
affiliated_organisations.role: Elite protective corps; grew into a state within a state under Heinrich Himmler. Controlled camps, Gestapo, Waffen-SS.

affiliated_organisations.name: Hitlerjugend (HJ)
affiliated_organisations.role: Hitler Youth — mandatory membership for German boys aged 14-18.

affiliated_organisations.name: Bund Deutscher Mädel (BDM)
affiliated_organisations.role: League of German Girls — female counterpart to HJ.

affiliated_organisations.name: Deutsche Arbeitsfront (DAF)
affiliated_organisations.role: German Labour Front — replaced trade unions dissolved in 1933.

affiliated_organisations.name: Nationalsozialistische Volkswohlfahrt (NSV)
affiliated_organisations.role: National Socialist People's Welfare — administered social welfare.
